speaker: charlie
text: [Australian accent] Time for our science segment, and today we've got a breakthrough that's literally defying the laws of physics! Scientists have observed electrons in graphene flowing like a nearly frictionless liquid - it's the kind of exotic quantum state that could revolutionise technology as we know it.

speaker: alice
text: [Australian accent] That's wild, Alex! And here's another brain-bender - researchers have also found a way to supercharge our immune system's T cells by blocking a specific protein, making them more powerful at hunting down cancer cells. It's like giving our body's security team a major upgrade!

speaker: charlie
text: [Australian accent] Plus, we've got news from space that'll blow your mind - NASA's Artemis II mission just completed its historic lunar flyby, with the crew capturing stunning shots of Earth setting over the moon's horizon. It's the first time humans have been beyond low Earth orbit since 1972!
